commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: network-first for HTML to prevent stale index.html on refresh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite PWA OAuth: unified wait on all channels, always relay

Root cause: iOS PWA overlay returns non-null window.opener but
postMessage silently fails, causing deadlock. Fix by removing
isPWAStandalone() branching — listen on postMessage, relay polling,
and visibility change simultaneously. Callback always POSTs to
relay as backup. Added [oauth] logging throughout for debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v3 requires azure/login action, v2 works with publish-profile directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: generalize skill (no hardcoded paths), PPTX speaker notes support

PIPELINE_DIR is now resolved dynamically at runtime:
  1. EDUCATIONAL_VIDEO_PIPELINE env var
  2. Current directory (if it contains remotion.config.ts)
  3. ~/.claude/plugins/educational-video/config.json (saved on first run)
  4. Common install locations search
  5. Ask user, then save for next time
All commands in SKILL.md use $PIPELINE_DIR — no machine-specific paths.

PPTX notes extraction (pptx-to-images.ps1):
  Exports slide-notes.json alongside PNGs with speaker notes per slide.
  Claude asks whether to use existing notes or generate fresh narration.

PPTX notes write-back (pptx-add-notes.ps1):
  Offered at Phase 7 — appends narration to each slide's notes in the
  original PPTX. Backs up the file before writing.

commit.message:
fix: replace undefined cg.zeroany with {a_int:0}, remove C free() no-ops

- 29 sites: cg.zeroany → { a_int: 0 } (C's zero-initialized anything union)
  cg global was never defined in JS, causing ReferenceError in menu code
- spell.js: (selected, 0) → comment (C free, JS garbage collects)
- invent.js unsortloot: (loot_array_p, 0) → comment
- mkobj.js free_omonst: (m, 0) → comment, OMONST = null (not 0)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tune: slow heading curriculum + rate-limit goal expansion

Heading curriculum: 20%→60% of training
  Robot starts facing goal, gradually randomizes over 48k iters (not 16k).
  Learn to walk first, then learn to turn from arbitrary yaw.

Goal expansion: add 200-iter cooldown + tighter threshold 0.30→0.25
  Prevents rapid jump (v12 jumped 1.0→4.8m in 50 iters from stag noise).
  At most one +0.2m expansion per 200 iters.

commit.message:
Fix P2/P3 audit findings: activity code splits, bead defaults, and comp pad welding

- Externals: add comp pad fillet + nozzle bore weld volume AND time calculations
- Legacy engine: add back-gouge fill volume for single-bevel nozzle joints
- Longwelds/circwelds: fix SUBLON/MANLON and SUBCIR/MANCIR binary split —
  now properly splits inside (manual) vs outside (SAW) weld time
- Longwelds/circwelds: fix isSubArc logic that miscategorised FCAW/GMAW as
  sub-arc; only SAW is true sub-arc
- Longwelds/circwelds: add Math.max(0) guard on single-V insideDepth to
  prevent negative depth when rootFace >= shellThickness
- SAW bead size defaults: 4×12mm → 5×15mm (75mm² realistic for sub-arc)
- Thick nozzle preset: increase activity times ~1.5× above medium preset
  (was identical to medium — heavy nozzles need more cut/fit/NDE time)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Output dir always created; print_tree depth limit added
- CI: genisoimage installed on Ubuntu runners

Inline full TorchQuant source in llms.txt for LLM accessibility

The examples/ directory isn't part of the pip package, so coding agents
can't read those files from an installed reqm. Inlining the complete
TorchQuant class in llms.txt ensures any agent that reads the doc gets
the full recipe, with a link to the GitHub repo for the broader example.

commit.message:
Fix agent hallucination: fetch ADO diffs, add token budgeting, block speculation

- ADO adapter now fetches actual file content via Items API and computes
  unified diffs with difflib (was returning empty patches for all files)
- Context builder allocates diff token budget (120k default, configurable)
  with priority: security surface > blast radius > hotspots > rest
- Files with missing patches get explicit "[diff not available]" markers
- Large diffs get truncated with "[diff truncated — N lines omitted]"
- Anti-speculation evidence rules added to all agent output schemas

commit.message:
fix: output dir creation and print_tree depth guard

- Always call create_dir_all for the output directory regardless of the
  path value. The previous `!= "."` guard meant `./`, `./subdir/../`,
  or any other path equivalent to current dir would silently skip
  creation, then fail when writing files into a missing path.

- Add a MAX_TREE_DEPTH (100) guard to print_tree to prevent stack
  overflow on adversarially deep or circular filesystem trees.

commit.message:
Add send policy and /query endpoint for on-demand PGN requests (#55)

* Add PGN 59904 on-demand query support via POST /query and lplexc client

- Add POST /query HTTP endpoint that sends an ISO Request (PGN 59904) to
  the CAN bus and waits for a matching response frame, with configurable
  timeout (default 2s). Returns 504 on timeout, 503 if tx queue is full.
- Add public Broker.SendISORequest method (error-returning variant of the
  existing internal sendISORequest).
- Add lplexc.Client.RequestPGN for on-demand PGN queries via POST /query.
- Add lplexc.Client.Values and DecodedValues for GET /values endpoints.
- Add PGNValue, DeviceValues, DecodedPGNValue, DecodedDeviceValues types
  to lplexc/types.go.
- Add tests for all new functionality (server and client).
- Update README, CLAUDE.md, website HTTP API and Go client docs.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HVhxiJQQKKNgkvGZgg3wmX

* Add send policy to gate /send and /query endpoints

The /send and /query HTTP endpoints are now disabled by default and must
be explicitly enabled via -send-enabled (or send.enabled in HOCON config).
Optional allowlists restrict which PGNs (-send-allowed-pgns) and
destination CAN NAMEs (-send-allowed-names) may be targeted. Broadcast
destinations (255) bypass the NAME allowlist. Internal device discovery
via ISO requests at startup is unaffected.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HVhxiJQQKKNgkvGZgg3wmX

* Replace flat send allowlists with ordered rule-based DSL

Replace -send-allowed-pgns and -send-allowed-names flat flags with a
rule-based DSL via -send-rules (CLI, semicolon-separated) or send.rules
(HOCON, string list). Each rule is "[!] [pgn:<spec>] [name:<hex>,...]"
where PGN specs support values, comma-separated lists, and ranges.
Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom with first-match-wins semantics.
! prefix creates deny rules. Omitting pgn: or name: acts as wildcard.
No matching rule = deny. Empty rules + enabled = allow all.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HVhxiJQQKKNgkvGZgg3wmX

* Support native HOCON object format for send rules

In addition to the existing string DSL format, send.rules in HOCON config
files now accepts native objects: { deny, pgn, name } where name can be a
single string or an array. Both string and object forms can be mixed in the
same array. CLI -send-rules flag continues to use semicolon-separated DSL.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HVhxiJQQKKNgkvGZgg3wmX

* Fix uint32 overflow in test: use variable for negative int32-to-uint32 conversion

Go constant folding rejects uint32(int32(-1223321000)) at compile time.
Using a variable forces the conversion to happen at runtime.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent panics from malformed ISO/UDF data

Three crash vectors fixed:

1. iso9660.rs: bounds check was `< 33` but data[33] was accessed when
   filename_length > 0. Changed to `< 34` and added an explicit check
   that `33 + filename_length <= data.len()`.

2. udf.rs: `alloc.extents[0]` panicked for files with inline data
   (ad_type 3), where the extents vec is intentionally empty. Now uses
   `extents.first()` and falls back to a size-only TreeNode for inline
   files (cat/extract will report "location not available", not crash).

3. udf.rs: `map_length` in partition map loop could be 0 (malformed
   LVD), causing `map_offset += 0` to loop forever. Added a `break`
   guard when map_length == 0.
